2604.16917.title: x1: Learning to Think Adaptively Across Languages and Cultures
2604.16917.paper_summary: Languages encode distinct abstractions and inductive priors, yet most large language models (LLMs) overlook this diversity by reasoning in a single dominant language. In this work, we introduce x1, a family of reasoning models that can adaptively reason in an advantageous language on a per-instance basis. To isolate the effect of reasoning-language choice, x1 is constructed without expanding the model's knowledge boundaries and is trained by contrasting linguistically distinct reasoning trajectories for the same input. Our extensive experiments demonstrate the benefits of adaptive multilingual reasoning across multilingual mathematical reasoning and culturally grounded tasks. Moreover, our results challenge a simplistic view of scaling laws: while scaling reduces cross-lingual disparities in procedural domains such as math reasoning, it does not eliminate the advantages of culture-associated languages in culturally grounded tasks, as we empirically show that such reasoning enables more efficient and accurate cultural knowledge recall. Overall, our findings establish language choice as a functional component of reasoning, with implications for building more generalist and globally competent reasoning models.
2604.16917.paper_summary_zh: 語言編碼了不同的抽象概念和歸納先驗，但大多數大型語言模型（LLMs）卻忽視了這種多樣性，僅以單一主導語言進行推理。在本研究中，我們介紹了 x1，一系列推理模型，能夠根據每個實例自適應地在有利的語言中進行推理。為了隔離推理語言選擇的影響，x1 的構建並未擴展模型的知識邊界，而是通過對相同輸入進行語言上不同的推理軌跡對比來訓練。我們的廣泛實驗展示了在多語言數學推理和文化根植任務中，自適應多語言推理的好處。此外，我們的結果挑戰了對擴展法則的簡化看法：儘管擴展減少了數學推理等程序性領域中的跨語言差異，但它並未消除文化相關語言在文化根植任務中的優勢，因為我們實證表明這種推理能夠更有效且準確地回憶文化知識。總體而言，我們的研究結果確立了語言選擇作為推理的一個功能組成部分，對於構建更通用和具全球競爭力的推理模型具有重要意義。
2604.16917.author: Yangfan Ye et.al.
2604.16917.authors: Yangfan Ye, Xiaocheng Feng, Xiachong Feng, Yichong Huang, Zekun Yuan, Lei Huang, Weitao Ma, Qichen Hong, Yunfei Lu, Dandan Tu, Bing Qin
